ocr.text: In the context of global population distribution, the data presented in the accompanying table highlights the demographic
landscape as of 2010. The table categorizes populations by unit and region, providing a clear representation of the estimated
populations in millions for Brazil, the USA, and the combined figures for China and India, alongside the total world population.
Notably, the population of Brazil was recorded at 201.1 million, accounting for approximately 2.9% of the global population.
Unit  Brazil  USA  China/India  World
Vehicles per habitant  0.159  0.773  0.0394  0.148
Gasoline consumption, 2008 (thousand Barrels per day)1  327.1 (1.5%)  8,989.2 (42.2%)  1,693.5 (7.9%)  21,323.1
Fuel ethanol production, 2010 (thousand Barrels per day)1  486.0 (32.0%)  867.4 (57.1%)  42.0 (2.7%)  1520
Fuel ethanol consumption, 2010 (thousand Barrels per day)1  381.9 (26.9%)  838.8 (59.1%)  38 (2.7%)  1,418.5
Total Coal Consumption (Quadrillion Btu, 2010)1  0.5 (0.3%)  20.8 (13.7%)  86.1 (56.8%)  151.5
Total Petroleum Consumption ( Quadrillion Btu, 2010)1  5.2  36.0  25.5  175.0
Total Petroleum Consumption (Thousand Barrels Per Day, 2010)1  2,560 (2.9%)  19,180.1 (22.0%)  12,507.9 (14.3%)  87,213
Total Primary Energy Consumption (Quadrillion Btu, 2009)1  10.3 (2.1%)  94.5 (19.6%)  111.9 (23.2%)  483.0
Total Carbon Dioxide Emissions from the Consumption of Energy (Million Metric Tons, 2010)1  453.9 (1.4%)  5,610.1 (17.7%)  10,016.6 (31.5%)  31,780.4
In contrast, the USA's population stood at 310.2 million, representing 4.5% of the world total. The most striking figures emerge
from the collective populations of China and India, which amounted to 2,503.2 million, constituting a significant 36.5% of the
world's total population of 6,853 million. This data underscores not only the substantial demographic weight held by these
populous nations but also highlights the uneven distribution of population across different regions. Understanding these
population dynamics is crucial for policymakers and stakeholders when addressing global challenges such as resource
allocation, urban planning, and sustainability efforts in a rapidly changing world.
